name: Chili in a Bread Bowl
recipeIngredient: 1 can (16 oz.) kidney beans, rinsed and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, brown the ground beef and onion; drain excess fat.
2. Add kidney beans, tomato sauce, diced tomatoes (with juice), chili powder, and garlic powder to the pan. Stir to combine.
3. Simmer the mixture uncovered for 20 minutes.
4. Grease an 9-inch heart-shaped or round baking pan with oil.
5. In a separate saucepan over medium heat, bring water and butter to a boil. Gradually add flour, Parmesan cheese, and baking powder, stirring constantly until a smooth ball forms.
6. Remove the dough from heat and beat in eggs one at a time, continuing to stir until the mixture is smooth and shiny.
7. Pour the batter into the prepared pan, slightly building up the edges.
8. Bake at 425°F (220°C) for 25-30 minutes or until the center is firm and puffed, and the edges are golden brown.
9. Make a shallow slit in the center of the bread bowl to allow steam to escape.
10. Cool for 5 minutes before removing from the oven. Fill with chili and serve immediately.
Yield: 6 servings (Chili recipe makes more than will fit in the bread bowl.)
